questions: What is the exact Supabase deployment path for migrations in this workspace now: copy staged SQL into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` and run `supabase db push`, or use a different project/link command?
Which concrete Discord channel should receive the trace embed in prod, and should it be the same channel as `#daily-updates` or a separate operator/debug channel?
Should legacy suppression use scheduler-level routing only, publish-call suppression only, or both with scheduler-level primary routing plus a publisher safety flag?
What is the preferred publishing toggle name and deployment mechanism: environment variable, DB config row, or existing feature-flag/config pattern?
For the one prod sanity replay, is it acceptable to write topic rows/transitions with publishing off, or should replay run in a dry-run transaction/fake storage and leave prod topic tables unchanged?

success_criteria.criterion: The Phase-1 schema migration creates `topics`, `topic_sources`, `topic_aliases`, `topic_transitions`, `editorial_observations`, and the chosen run/checkpoint lease support with required uniqueness and indexes.

Backfill is idempotent and produces topic counts where posted topics are at least legacy `live_update_feed_items` count and watching topics are at least legacy `live_update_watchlist` count for the target environment, allowing only explained post-backfill deltas.
`TopicEditor.run_once()` can execute with publishing disabled, consume archived messages from the new checkpoint, write accepted topic state to new tables, and avoid Discord publishing calls.

All four dispatcher invariants are covered by tests: simple-post structural rejection, collision rejection/override, `(run_id, tool_call_id)` idempotency, and `(topic_id, message_id)` source uniqueness.
Every dispatcher rejection path writes a `topic_transitions` row with the locked `rejected_*` action shape and enough payload to identify the failed tool call and reason.
`render_topic()` is pure and has tests for simple, sectioned, story-update, and partial-publish failure/update behavior.
Scheduled runs, startup `--summary-now`, and owner `summarynow` route to the new topic editor by default, while explicitly named legacy/backfill routes remain available for rollback.
Legacy `live_update_editor.py` and `live_update_prompts.py` are not materially modified except for invocation suppression or explicit legacy compatibility marking.
Existing legacy live-update tests either pass unchanged or are clearly marked/updated as legacy compatibility tests without deleting rollback coverage.

A publishing-off sanity replay over the latest prod source-message window completes and produces a trace showing source count, tool calls, outcomes, rejections/overrides, latency, and would-publish messages.
Publishing flip has a documented rollback that mirrors the new checkpoint back to `live_update_checkpoints.last_message_id` before re-enabling legacy invocation.
The new editor code remains organized around deterministic helpers for canonicalization, dispatch, and rendering rather than burying those behaviors in the LLM prompt.
Cost/latency metadata is recorded per run so post-flip regressions are visible without log scraping.
Operator-only runbook values are filled before prod cutover: toggle name, suppression mechanism, bot identities, trace channel, and run-source discriminator.

assumptions: The locked decisions in `docs/live-update-editor-sprint-plan.md` supersede the older v6 redesign’s week-long shadow-mode gate, defer audit, and separate Phase-2 timeline.
The new implementation should live primarily in `src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py`, with optional small companion modules only if tests or readability justify them.
The existing `src/common/llm/claude_client.py` exposes the underlying Anthropic async client, so native tool-use can be implemented without a broad LLM wrapper refactor.
The legacy editor and prompt modules remain rollback implementation details and should not receive the new topic-editor logic.
The migration can use `.migrations_staging/` first, then be copied/applied to the workspace-level Supabase project following the repository’s current staging convention.
Publishing is disabled by default until the explicit prod flip, even after the new editor is wired as primary.
A run-source discriminator can be stored in `live_update_editor_runs.metadata` unless the schema migration chooses a dedicated column.
The checkpoint owner will be a separate `topic_editor_checkpoints` table unless implementation evidence strongly favors a column on `topic_editor_runs`.
